what_it_does: Preserves three verbatim voice-transcribed messages from Tim dated 2026-06-17, supplies the context they responded to, maps each message to existing project artifacts/tasks, and records related later transmissions from the same arc.
observations: The file stores Tim's three dense messages as verbatim source, with bracketed corrections for voice-transcription errors.
It frames the verbatim text as ground truth that future decompression must derive from.
Message 1 is mapped to board://item-a43918e1, task #73, build-prep/trigger-system/, and COVERAGE-RUNBOOK.md.
Message 2 is mapped to THE-ONE-SYSTEM.md sections 'IT APPLIES EVERYWHERE' and 'LEGIBILITY ARCHITECTURE'.
Message 3 is mapped to task #69 and board://item-501c4188 (MCP gaps).
Related later transmissions about coverage methodology and the one-application concept are appended with their own artifact mappings.
No code symbols are present; all references are narrative.
standouts: Tim requests a reusable trigger/hook registry connected to graph/MCP/routing and launchable Claude Code units.
He affirms that self-describing legibility architecture applies everywhere and derives from his seed equation/unifying theorem.
He flags two recall gaps: a missing self parameter and the lack of COMPANY_SESSION_ID injection for sessions.
He suggests self ID auto-resolution via channel registry as a possible rule.
He proposes a sub-agent coverage scan that uses the directory as a graph rather than a search for pre-imagined findings.
concerns: The source is a voice transcript with acknowledged phonetic errors, so interpretation is required.
Multiple distinct workstreams (triggers, legibility, recall gaps) are packed into one file, which may blur boundaries.
All artifact mappings are asserted narratively without machine-verifiable links.
Tim explicitly states he cannot see code or channels, so the instructions are high-level and need translation.
conventions: Uses markdown headers and blockquotes to separate verbatim source from interpretive context.
Labels bracketed corrections inside the verbatim text.
Explicitly declares the verbatim section as ground truth for later decompression.
Cross-references project board items and task numbers inline.
Breaks terse log conventions by embedding dense, multi-message context and later addenda.
notes: A preservation log of Tim's three dense 2026-06-17 transmissions, mapping them to existing project artifacts and flagging trigger registry, legibility architecture, and recall-gap work.
questions: What is the exact statement of the 'seed equation' and 'unifying theorem'?
What is the intended API or data model for the trigger/hook registry?
Which specific channel registry and recall system are referenced?
How is COMPANY_SESSION_ID currently generated and where should injection happen?
What does 'dynamic workflow' refer to in this codebase?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file preserves verbatim transmissions, adds context, and maps each to project artifacts and tasks as stated.
apparent_themes: Verbatim preservation as ground truth for intent.
Composability and reusability of triggers and agent launches.
Union of multiple streams into one application.
Coverage as address-space allocation rather than search.
intention_signals: Create a durable, interpretive ledger entry that anchors Tim's high-level intent in his own words, links it to actionable project memory, and instructs downstream agents to decompress from this source to avoid drift.
novelty: Treats a founder's voice transcript as canonical source material for system intent, with explicit 'decompress from here' instructions and bracketed phonetic corrections.
contribution: Provides a verbatim, contextualized record of founder intent and its mapping to existing tasks and artifacts, which a fused system can use to ground interpretations and trace requirement lineage.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a verbatim preservation and contextual mapping of Tim's three 2026-06-17 transmissions about a reusable trigger/hook registry, self-describing legibility architecture, and recall/identity gaps, linking them to project board items, tasks, and related design documents.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to serve as ground-truth source material so downstream agents could decompress Tim's intent accurately and route the resulting work into project memory.
